unit_02 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_02

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 353 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
In Japanese aesthetics, especially but not only in Noh, beauty contains the idea of __________: beauty must have an air of evanescence, the intimation of its own demise.In Japanese aesthetics, especially but not only in Noh, beauty contains the idea of __________: beauty must have an air of evanescence, the intimation of its own demise.
答案：C, F　decay; deterioration
aestheticaesthetics
aesthetic: standard unit headword | aesthetics: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
For many adults, the adolescent years occupy (i)__________ place in the memory, which to some degree is even quantifiable: give a grown adult a series of random prompts and cues, and odds are he or she will recall (ii)__________ number of memories from adolescence.For many adults, the adolescent years occupy (i)__________ place in the memory, which to some degree is even quantifiable: give a grown adult a series of random prompts and cues, and odds are he or she will recall (ii)__________ number of memories from adolescence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　a privileged; a disproportionate
disproportionateprompt
disproportionate: standard unit headword | prompt: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
An innovative musician, accompanist extraordinaire, and protomodern hipster, Lester Young (1909-1959) remains a (i)__________ in the jazz firmament. Some of the reasons are obvious: although he dressed with conspicuous cool, he chose an artistic trajectory that seemed calculated to (ii)__________ the spotlight--his best work was in the bands of other musicians, such as Billie Holiday and Count Basie, rather than at the front of his own. Moreover, he was shy, subtle, and sensitive--traits not usually associated with great (iii)__________.An innovative musician, accompanist extraordinaire, and protomodern hipster, Lester Young (1909-1959) remains a (i)__________ in the jazz firmament. Some of the reasons are obvious: although he dressed with conspicuous cool, he chose an artistic trajectory that seemed calculated to (ii)__________ the spotlight--his best work was in the bands of other musicians, such as Billie Holiday and Count Basie, rather than at the front of his own. Moreover, he was shy, subtle, and sensitive--traits not usually associated with great (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　lesser light; shun; celebrity
traitconspicuous
trait: standard unit headword | conspicuous: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors’ attempts to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors’ attempts to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.
答案：B　stalwart
ficklesolicitous
fickle: standard unit headword | solicitous: standard unit headword
#5 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　smothering; unquestioningly embrace
hazardconversely
hazard: standard unit headword | conversely: standard unit headword
#6 SE
His own writing style was __________: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.His own writing style was __________: colorful and tart in its choice of language, willing to run risks in its allusions, metaphors, and verbal juxtapositions, prone to irreverent conclusions designed to surprise or startle.
答案：B, D　audacious; striking
effusiveaudacious

#7 TC TRIPLE
Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at
retaliatedelineate
retaliate: standard unit headword | delineate: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
Geologists suggest that as the most productive oil reservoirs begin to dry up, the expensive cost and high risk of drilling in the marginal area becomes less __________ and more acceptable.Geologists suggest that as the most productive oil reservoirs begin to dry up, the expensive cost and high risk of drilling in the marginal area becomes less __________ and more acceptable.
答案：A　onerous
efficaciousreservoir

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　scrutinized; revelations
counterscrutinize
counter: standard unit headword | scrutinize: standard unit headword

The Great Lakes wolf is a __________, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.The Great Lakes wolf is a __________, stumping scientists as to whether it is a subspecies of the gray wolf or a distinct species.
答案：B, F　riddle; conundrum
prototypeparagon
prototype: standard unit headword | paragon: standard unit headword
#11 TC TRIPLE
The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly
gallgalling
gall: standard unit headword | galling: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：B, D　intransigence; autonomy

The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　embarrassing; ingenuity; established

#14 TC DOUBLE

Blank 2
答案：C, D　unaltered in the process; reflective of

The artist is known for making photographs that deals with political matters, yet because her art is open-ended and evocative, it would be wrong to characterize it as __________.The artist is known for making photographs that deals with political matters, yet because her art is open-ended and evocative, it would be wrong to characterize it as __________.
答案：A　polemical
evocative
evocative: standard unit headword
#16 TC SINGLE
Scientists have argued not only that the chains of atoms called ladder compounds have __________ theoretical interest but also that studies of such systems can lead to important practical applications.Scientists have argued not only that the chains of atoms called ladder compounds have __________ theoretical interest but also that studies of such systems can lead to important practical applications.
答案：E　intrinsic
intrinsic
intrinsic: standard unit headword
#17 SE

答案：D, E　destroys; annihilates

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　idealized; discrepant; visionary

#19 SE
Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ __________ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.Films that critics have slumbered through rarely generate industry excitement, even though the critics’ __________ reception may be less the fault of the movie than of its unfortunate time slot near a fatiguing film festival’s conclusion.
答案：A, C　somnolent; lethargic
lethargic

Blank 3

答案：B　severe

答案：C, F　insufficient; meager
nonetheless

答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude
progression

#29 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
chaotic
chaotic: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　laudatory; a deified; debunk
personage

Blank 2
答案：A, E　demurred; assuage the qualms

In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　adopt; threatened to compromise; distinctiveness
uphold

Blank 2

答案：D　treasure hunting
theorize

答案：A, B　left to; ceded to
cede

#50 TC TRIPLE
Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.Native American art is not a (i) __________ enterprise: it reflects differences among artists' personal, community, and tribal experience. As Native American artists continue to create the art that is valid for them, however, the (ii) __________ in Native American art between traditional and modern styles (primarily promulgated by non-Native American critics) has become outmoded. There is heightened recognition that placing Native American art in these two opposing categories has had a (iii) __________ effect on both artists and viewers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　monolithic; supposed distinction; deleterious
promulgate
promulgate: standard unit headword
#51 TC DOUBLE
The nineteenth-century periodical The Farmer's Wife positioned the farm woman as a consumer, as evidenced by its echoing the (i) __________ sensibilities of consumer-oriented magazines such as Ladies' Home Journal, but in reality the farm woman was often engaged in (ii) __________ as much as in consumption, planting and harvesting alongside other workers in the field.The nineteenth-century periodical The Farmer's Wife positioned the farm woman as a consumer, as evidenced by its echoing the (i) __________ sensibilities of consumer-oriented magazines such as Ladies' Home Journal, but in reality the farm woman was often engaged in (ii) __________ as much as in consumption, planting and harvesting alongside other workers in the field.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　acquisitive; production
echo
echo: standard unit headword

Even though no other species in the Rana genus of frogs exhibits such __________ habits, once crawfish frogs have finally been located, they are relatively easy to film.Even though no other species in the Rana genus of frogs exhibits such __________ habits, once crawfish frogs have finally been located, they are relatively easy to film.
答案：A　furtive
furtive
furtive: standard unit headword

答案：B, D　economy; concision
evoke
evoke: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　obviates; perpetuate

Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.Despite the occasional (i)__________ of their venues, the culture of corporate conferences is a deeply (ii)__________ one, each day consisted of nearly nine hours of continuous lectures and panels enlivened by pleasantries or anything that could be construed as a joke. The only (iii)__________ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　opulence; ascetic; respite from

seclusion: standard unit headword

Although many skeptics of the scientific theory __________ critiques that have long since been disproved, some of the doubters arguably bring up valid points.Although many skeptics of the scientific theory __________ critiques that have long since been disproved, some of the doubters arguably bring up valid points.
答案：C, F　recycle; rehash

答案：C　unimpeachable
worrisome
worrisome: standard unit headword
#58 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)__________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii)__________ a message as securely, according to a physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii)__________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane

intercept: standard unit headword

答案：A, E　limpid; pellucid

The humor in this play derives from its (i)__________. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.The humor in this play derives from its (i)__________. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)__________ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　verbal nimbleness; plodding

While many books have emphasized the differences between Delaney and his father - and his need to differentiate himself from his father - Delaney takes pains in this memoir to __________ his closeness with his dad.While many books have emphasized the differences between Delaney and his father - and his need to differentiate himself from his father - Delaney takes pains in this memoir to __________ his closeness with his dad.
答案：A　underscore

#62 TC TRIPLE
For Plato the art of music was so (i)__________ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato's conception seems (ii)__________. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)__________ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)__________ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato's conception seems (ii)__________. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)__________ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
anchor
anchor: standard unit headword

Attending with equal (i) __________ to any question that presents itself, the sociologist's work has strengths and weaknesses that flow from this energetically (ii) __________ approach.Attending with equal (i) __________ to any question that presents itself, the sociologist's work has strengths and weaknesses that flow from this energetically (ii) __________ approach.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　fervor; indiscriminate
lethargy
lethargy: standard unit headword

Mary Beard notes that the classical past has never been __________ by only one political tendency or party: the classics have been used in support of revolutions as well as dictatorships.Mary Beard notes that the classical past has never been __________ by only one political tendency or party: the classics have been used in support of revolutions as well as dictatorships.
答案：E　co-opted

答案：A, B　diminutive; minuscular

#66 TC TRIPLE
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.

Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　an anomaly; pessimistic; obliterated

If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)__________ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)__________ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)__________ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)__________ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)__________ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)__________ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive

Some say he should have included more (ii)__________, but he is wise to let the fact speak for themselves. Some say he should have included more (ii)__________, but he is wise to let the fact speak for themselves. 答案：E　follow

embody: standard unit headword
#101 SE
Since some contemporary western dietitians believe that the only function of food is to provide nourishment, these dietitians view an emphasis on the aesthetic dimension of the culinary art as __________.Since some contemporary western dietitians believe that the only function of food is to provide nourishment, these dietitians view an emphasis on the aesthetic dimension of the culinary art as __________.
答案：B, D　unwarranted; groundless
aesthetic
aesthetic: standard unit headword
#102 TC DOUBLE
Collecting such fragment of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i)__________ artifacts into objective (ii)__________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.Collecting such fragment of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i)__________ artifacts into objective (ii)__________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　trivial; importance
trivial
trivial: standard unit headword
#103 TC SINGLE
Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating in the process.
答案：B　addiction to
credit
credit: standard unit headword
#104 TC TRIPLE
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)__________, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)__________, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious
intrinsic
intrinsic: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Few ideas are more __________ than the notion that cultures evolve in Darwin fashion; many academics have begun writing about cultural evolution, but few treat the underlying Darwinian logic with the care it deserves.Few ideas are more __________ than the notion that cultures evolve in Darwin fashion; many academics have begun writing about cultural evolution, but few treat the underlying Darwinian logic with the care it deserves.
答案：A, C　abused; misused
underlie
underlie: standard unit headword
#106 TC DOUBLE
Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i) __________ philosophy, (ii) __________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas: that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and given a measure of discretion over their own learning.Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i) __________ philosophy, (ii) __________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas: that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and given a measure of discretion over their own learning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an anomalous; embodies
embody
embody: standard unit headword
#107 TC SINGLE
Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating.Since the deficit predicament is fundamentally a long-term problem, the legislature’s __________ short-term approaches has actually compounded the difficulty in each succeeding year, eroding the state’s credit rating.
答案：B　addiction to
credit
credit: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage
nonetheless
nonetheless: standard unit headword
#109 SE
Although field studies have linked inbreeding to declines among song sparrow populations, some researchers argue that, in nature, inbreeding proves __________ as a factor when compared with crushing blows from weather changes.Although field studies have linked inbreeding to declines among song sparrow populations, some researchers argue that, in nature, inbreeding proves __________ as a factor when compared with crushing blows from weather changes.
答案：C, F　trivial; inconsequential
trivial
trivial: standard unit headword
#110 TC DOUBLE
The author of this political history text shows considerable bias against the political party when assigning credit or blame for its actions: he deems (i)__________ what he favors and avoidable what he (ii)__________.The author of this political history text shows considerable bias against the political party when assigning credit or blame for its actions: he deems (i)__________ what he favors and avoidable what he (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inevitable; condemns
credit
credit: standard unit headword
#111 TC SINGLE
A significant element of the Gothic genre, the literary grotesque is sometimes inaccurately discussed as if it were a __________ Gothic or, conversely, as if it were something entirely different from Gothic.A significant element of the Gothic genre, the literary grotesque is sometimes inaccurately discussed as if it were a __________ Gothic or, conversely, as if it were something entirely different from Gothic.
答案：A　synonym for
conversely
conversely: standard unit headword
#112 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating
chaotic
chaotic: standard unit headword
#113 SE
Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.Her attempts to wrest fiction free from traditional constraints like plot and character were never entirely popular with readers; nonetheless, her fiction has had __________ influence on critical theory, novel, cinema, and even psychology.
答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable
nonetheless
nonetheless: standard unit headword
#114 TC DOUBLE
It would be (i) __________ not to (ii) __________ these tabloid journalists for thriving in hard times: they deserve credit for doing well in a profession in financial straits.It would be (i) __________ not to (ii) __________ these tabloid journalists for thriving in hard times: they deserve credit for doing well in a profession in financial straits.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　churlish; admire
credit
credit: standard unit headword
#115 TC TRIPLE
Parker's model of human affairs reflects (i) __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii) __________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.Parker's model of human affairs reflects (i) __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii) __________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　a sanguine; pessimistic; diverge markedly
premise
premise: standard unit headword
#116 TC SINGLE
The best argument in favor of one space after a period over two is __________ one: one space is simpler, cleaner, and more visually pleasing.The best argument in favor of one space after a period over two is __________ one: one space is simpler, cleaner, and more visually pleasing.
答案：A　an aesthetic
aesthetic
aesthetic: standard unit headword
#117 SE
Because chemistry’s position as one of the natural sciences has long seemed __________, historians have generally treated the foundation of chemical professorship as an inevitable component of the progression of universities.Because chemistry’s position as one of the natural sciences has long seemed __________, historians have generally treated the foundation of chemical professorship as an inevitable component of the progression of universities.
答案：A, F　manifest; self-evident
progression
progression: standard unit headword
#118 TC DOUBLE
Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking
credulous
credulous: standard unit headword
#119 TC TRIPLE
Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i) __________, and at times even (ii) __________. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii) __________ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson's gazelles.Compared with, say, the precision of a skein of geese winging its way across the autumn sky in a V formation, the seasonal marches of grazers across the Serengeti seem (i) __________, and at times even (ii) __________. But, in fact, years of careful observation by scientists have shown that there is (iii) __________ those migrations of zebras, wildebeests, and Thomson's gazelles.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　disorganized; chaotic; a definite order to
chaotic
chaotic: standard unit headword
#120 TC SINGLE
Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal __________ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal __________ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.
答案：B　bemoaned
nonetheless
nonetheless: standard unit headword
#121 SE
Caricature can be revealing as well as amusing, and Ager’s novel is both: Ager’s delineation of class, ethnic, and generational struggle is exaggerated for comical effect, but it __________ nonetheless.Caricature can be revealing as well as amusing, and Ager’s novel is both: Ager’s delineation of class, ethnic, and generational struggle is exaggerated for comical effect, but it __________ nonetheless.
答案：A, E　resonates; rings true
nonetheless
nonetheless: standard unit headword
#122 TC DOUBLE
The history of the region's natural resources has been one of initial (i)__________ followed by (ii)__________; as such the region has over time gone from a resource-rich to a resource-dependent economy.The history of the region's natural resources has been one of initial (i)__________ followed by (ii)__________; as such the region has over time gone from a resource-rich to a resource-dependent economy.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　abundance; rapacious depletion
rapacious
rapacious: standard unit headword
#123 TC TRIPLE
During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)__________ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)__________ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)__________ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)__________ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)__________ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)__________ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　presided over; undisputed; insufficient
credit
credit: standard unit headword
#124 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not __________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not __________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
efficacy
efficacy: standard unit headword
#125 SE
Recent research runs counter to the long-cherished notion that a small drop in body temperature during and after surgery is either __________ or actually protects the patient by slowing metabolism and reducing the body’s demand for blood and oxygen.Recent research runs counter to the long-cherished notion that a small drop in body temperature during and after surgery is either __________ or actually protects the patient by slowing metabolism and reducing the body’s demand for blood and oxygen.
答案：B, C　immaterial; inconsequential
counter

Collecting such fragments of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i) __________ artifacts into objective (ii) __________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.Collecting such fragments of contemporary popular culture as postcards, newspaper clippings, and wallpaper patterns, Susan Hiller transforms these seemingly (i) __________ artifacts into objective (ii) __________ by making them the centerpieces in her compositions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　trivial; importance
trivial
trivial: standard unit headword
#127 TC TRIPLE
The Golden Gate Bridge has been quite (i)__________ by most aesthetic and functional criteria. However, as a structure it has had some limitations. Shortly after it opened, its roadway proved to be (ii)__________ under certain wind conditions, so it was stiffened. The additional steel that provided that stiffening naturally added weight to the structure, and this made it (iii)__________ later to add a proposed rail system beneath the roadway.The Golden Gate Bridge has been quite (i)__________ by most aesthetic and functional criteria. However, as a structure it has had some limitations. Shortly after it opened, its roadway proved to be (ii)__________ under certain wind conditions, so it was stiffened. The additional steel that provided that stiffening naturally added weight to the structure, and this made it (iii)__________ later to add a proposed rail system beneath the roadway.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　successful; overly flexible; impossible
aesthetic
aesthetic: standard unit headword
#128 TC SINGLE
__________ is a critical aspect of language development: a particular signal can perform different functions in different populations, with no relationship between the signal's intrinsic features and its meaning.__________ is a critical aspect of language development: a particular signal can perform different functions in different populations, with no relationship between the signal's intrinsic features and its meaning.
答案：B　Arbitrariness
intrinsic
intrinsic: standard unit headword
#129 SE
The author advocates a diminished role for philosophy, aiming to show that many of the questions traditionally debated among philosophers can be __________ the realm of scientific inquiry.The author advocates a diminished role for philosophy, aiming to show that many of the questions traditionally debated among philosophers can be __________ the realm of scientific inquiry.
答案：A, F　ceded to; left to
cede
cede: standard unit headword
#130 TC DOUBLE
Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i) __________ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) __________ such fundamental skills truly are.Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i) __________ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) __________ such fundamental skills truly are.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　failed to master; impressive
domain
domain: standard unit headword
#131 TC TRIPLE
Excitement is often considered anathema in science writing because it interjects the scientist's emotion and values into the work. Indeed, one should not be too quick to (i)__________ of science. At the same time, however, this (ii)__________ stance can occasionally be (iii)__________, cloaking more than it reveals.Excitement is often considered anathema in science writing because it interjects the scientist's emotion and values into the work. Indeed, one should not be too quick to (i)__________ of science. At the same time, however, this (ii)__________ stance can occasionally be (iii)__________, cloaking more than it reveals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　abandon the objectivity; dispassionate; misleading
anathema
anathema: standard unit headword
#132 SE
The action in Zadie Smith’s novel On Beauty is mediated by an unabashedly __________ narrator who does not hesitate to inform us, as once upon a time the narrators of novels were wont to do, how we behave in general and how society usually works.The action in Zadie Smith’s novel On Beauty is mediated by an unabashedly __________ narrator who does not hesitate to inform us, as once upon a time the narrators of novels were wont to do, how we behave in general and how society usually works.
答案：B, C　obtrusive; conspicuous
conspicuous
conspicuous: standard unit headword
#133 TC SINGLE
__________ in the ordinary sense-meticulous attention to whether one is doing the right thing-is a trait often praised by ethical theorists, who generally think that the more time spent on self-scrutiny, the better.__________ in the ordinary sense-meticulous attention to whether one is doing the right thing-is a trait often praised by ethical theorists, who generally think that the more time spent on self-scrutiny, the better.
答案：E　Conscientiousness
trait

#134 TC DOUBLE
The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i) __________. Some say he should have included more (ii) __________, but he is wise to let the facts speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would bog down the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.The author clearly supports the causes he writes about, but he is more a narrator than (i) __________. Some say he should have included more (ii) __________, but he is wise to let the facts speak for themselves. They are complex enough to prompt many kinds of interpretation, and he would bog down the complicated tale if he tried to adjudicate all of their competing claims.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　an advocate; analysis of events
prompt
prompt: standard unit headword
#135 TC TRIPLE
Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　alarming; reassuring; unexceptional
cascade
cascade: standard unit headword
#136 TC TRIPLE
Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself
prompt

答案：A　heterogeneous

答案：C, F　insufficient; meager
nonetheless

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　dubious; demur

答案：C　inscrutable

#141 SE
The potential reduction in water lost from the proposed reservoir from evaporation is __________ inconsequential: even the minimum projected reduction would save enough water to furnish the needs of a Denver-sized municipality.The potential reduction in water lost from the proposed reservoir from evaporation is __________ inconsequential: even the minimum projected reduction would save enough water to furnish the needs of a Denver-sized municipality.
答案：C, E　scarcely; hardly

#142 TC TRIPLE
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to use forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
standstill
standstill: standard unit headword

Witness the (ii) __________ a program that allows us to create computer greeting cards for the entire year in one sitting.

Blank 2

答案：C　confound

答案：D, E　preclude; rule out

The superintendent's subsequent vindication will have come as no surprise to them.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 1
Blank 2

#148 TC SINGLE
Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors' attempt to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.Anthropologist Jane Goodall was __________ in her determination to anthropomorphize the animals she observed with such empathy, and so resisted her editors' attempt to recast her descriptions in more dispassionate language.
答案：B　stalwart

答案：B, F　conspicuous; prominent

#150 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rampant; a requirement for

#151 TC TRIPLE
The newly discovered fossil skeletons have the fins, scales, and other attributes of a giant fish, but also the telling anatomical traits of (i) __________ creature, such as the beginnings of digits, protowrists, and joints resembling elbows toward the forward fins. The specimen is a fish with features that (ii) __________ the emergence of land animals and is thus a (iii) __________ of amphibians, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals.The newly discovered fossil skeletons have the fins, scales, and other attributes of a giant fish, but also the telling anatomical traits of (i) __________ creature, such as the beginnings of digits, protowrists, and joints resembling elbows toward the forward fins. The specimen is a fish with features that (ii) __________ the emergence of land animals and is thus a (iii) __________ of amphibians, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　a transitional; anticipate; predecessor

#152 TC TRIPLE
Persian prose writers from the second half of the twelfth century onward were characteristically (i)__________ the literary form of their works, a fact that some scholars have perceived as a derogation of those works' content. It may be better interpreted as (ii)__________ the development of an awareness of authorship, for the awareness of authorship (iii)__________ the awareness of form.Persian prose writers from the second half of the twelfth century onward were characteristically (i)__________ the literary form of their works, a fact that some scholars have perceived as a derogation of those works' content. It may be better interpreted as (ii)__________ the development of an awareness of authorship, for the awareness of authorship (iii)__________ the awareness of form.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　preoccupied with; an indication of; evolves through
preoccupy
preoccupy: standard unit headword
#153 TC SINGLE
Among the Meakambut people of Papua New Guinea, legends are associated with specific caves in the Sepik region, and the legends are __________: only cave owner can share its secrets.Among the Meakambut people of Papua New Guinea, legends are associated with specific caves in the Sepik region, and the legends are __________: only cave owner can share its secrets.
答案：A　proprietary
immutable
immutable: standard unit headword
#154 SE
Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede
underlie
underlie: standard unit headword

Throughout much of the twentieth century, common scientific sense seemed to dictate that animals could not make a choice based on rational or aesthetic criteria. Such choices were (i) __________ the mental capacity of humans. Scientists who (ii) __________ this animal-human cognitive division were often accused of anthropomorphism.Throughout much of the twentieth century, common scientific sense seemed to dictate that animals could not make a choice based on rational or aesthetic criteria. Such choices were (i) __________ the mental capacity of humans. Scientists who (ii) __________ this animal-human cognitive division were often accused of anthropomorphism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　reserved for; transgressed

Those who blame the inadequacies of science education for students' lack of reasoning skills perpetuate their unproductive notion of science as (i) __________, unique in its capacity to inoculate us against superstition and ignorance. Certainly a good science education can (ii) __________ habits of mind, but the (iii) __________ effect of education in nonscientific, humanistic subjects such as literature and history should not be underrated.Those who blame the inadequacies of science education for students' lack of reasoning skills perpetuate their unproductive notion of science as (i) __________, unique in its capacity to inoculate us against superstition and ignorance. Certainly a good science education can (ii) __________ habits of mind, but the (iii) __________ effect of education in nonscientific, humanistic subjects such as literature and history should not be underrated.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　salvational; counter uncritical; salutary
counter
counter: standard unit headword

答案：A　specious

答案：D, E　preclude; rule out

Blank 2
答案：A, E　demurred; assuage the qualms

答案：B　documentary

答案：B, D　checking; stemming

To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.

Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview

答案：E　credibility

Because Mars is smaller than Earth, it cooled faster, and therefore it lost the interrial heat that drives tectonic activity.The reason geological activity on Mars is so (i) __________ while on Earth it remains (ii) __________ is that Earth's diameter is about twice the size of Mars. Because Mars is smaller than Earth, it cooled faster, and therefore it lost the interrial heat that drives tectonic activity.

Blank 2
答案：B, D　moribund; vital

Instead, a tangle of intuitions, know-how, factual knowledge, and a variety of other mental constructs allows learners to entertain choices and make decisions. Nonetheless, (i) __________ such mental activity (ii) __________ is (iii) __________: in many situations this process works, although it can prove to be wrong as a result of overgeneralizations.When learners are engaged in mathematical problem-solving, their activity does not have from the outset the structure of what will be considered a mathematical proof. Instead, a tangle of intuitions, know-how, factual knowledge, and a variety of other mental constructs allows learners to entertain choices and make decisions. Nonetheless, (i) __________ such mental activity (ii) __________ is (iii) __________: in many situations this process works, although it can prove to be wrong as a result of overgeneralizations.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　describing; as confused; uncharitable

Although that combination of personality traits is not uncommon, it is rare for a book that is so transparently an exercise in (iii)__________ to have the exact opposite of its intended effect.

This apparent (i) __________ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii) __________, noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.Rocks of the Morrison Formation in the western United States yield many dinosaur fossils but few plant fossils. This apparent (i) __________ of plants leads some scientists to speculate that the region was once mostly barren. But others have (ii) __________, noting that the herbivorous dinosaurs there would have needed an abundant food supply.

Blank 2

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) __________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading

In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.

Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amenable to refutation; upended by; persisted

It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) __________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) __________ children whose nature is to be inactive.The study found that every child has his or her own highly (i) __________ daily level of activity. It is not affected by school physical education, or whether the child walks or drives to school, or how much time the child spends in front of a television. We don't know what determines this (ii) __________ activity level. But engineering the environment to require more activity (iii) __________ children whose nature is to be inactive.

His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　restraining; plausible; blunder

There, “something” and “nothing” are (ii) __________: it is entirely consistent for there to be nothing, and nonetheless for stuff to show up when we try to detect it.One example of a (i) __________ occurs in a vacuum state in quantum field theory. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty

Instead, his work suggests that the photographer simply cannot ignore so much of the built American landscape but is obligated to (ii)__________ what we pass through day in and day out, regardless of (iii)__________.Just because, as a photographer, Friedlander (i)__________ places that most people consider ugly does not mean that he is out to prove they are beautiful. Instead, his work suggests that the photographer simply cannot ignore so much of the built American landscape but is obligated to (ii)__________ what we pass through day in and day out, regardless of (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2

Meteorology is one of the few fields of applied science that demands prediction; since prediction involves considerable uncertainty and uncertainty is __________ scientists, meteorology will continue to exist in a fraught intellectual space.Meteorology is one of the few fields of applied science that demands prediction; since prediction involves considerable uncertainty and uncertainty is __________ scientists, meteorology will continue to exist in a fraught intellectual space.
答案：B, E　anathema to; repellent to

答案：A　intelligible
undervalue

#201 TC DOUBLE
The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i) __________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii) __________ public health.The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i) __________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii) __________ public health.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　inadequate; can emerge too late to protect
hazard

#202 TC TRIPLE
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i) __________. Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii) __________ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii) __________ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry's bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility
proportionrelinquish
proportion: standard unit headword | relinquish: standard unit headword
#203 SE
The aesthetic appeal of Sydney's Hyde Park Obelisk was often __________ its practicality: the structure was not just an impressive display of cosmopolitan splendor but a means to vent gases from the city's underground sewer system.The aesthetic appeal of Sydney's Hyde Park Obelisk was often __________ its practicality: the structure was not just an impressive display of cosmopolitan splendor but a means to vent gases from the city's underground sewer system.
答案：B, F　a distraction from; a disguise for
aesthetic
aesthetic: standard unit headword
#204 TC SINGLE
According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from __________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from __________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.
答案：E　partiality
lethargy
lethargy: standard unit headword
#205 TC DOUBLE
Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.Conservationists tend to be (i) __________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) __________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　uncomfortable with; utilitarian
intrinsic
intrinsic: standard unit headword
#206 TC TRIPLE
The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.The guarantee of academic freedom to professors is intended to encourage intellectual independence. But so long as faculty members receive training in a specific academic discipline and are appointed to positions defined by discipline, and so long as colleges and universities are organized by departments that (i) __________ long-standing disciplinary traditions, academic freedom will also (ii) __________ unique discipline-specific expectations. Yet the right to (iii) __________ disciplinary paradigms is also fundamental to academic freedom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　inherit; tend to embody; resist
delineateembody
delineate: standard unit headword | embody: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　a derisive; a contemptuous

答案：E　a protean

Blank 1
Blank 2

Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. There are also academics who (iii) __________ the removal of retouches and even of overpaintings, holding that even though they may date from years after the original composition, they embody history in themselves.Thorough cleanings or restorations that try to return much-loved old master paintings to their original condition (i) __________. Quite apart from a reasonable wariness of paint loss, sheer loyalty to the (ii) __________ look of a work, dirt and all, prejudices many. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　invariably face opposition; accustomed; militate against

#211 SE
One object of examining past medical practices is to identify useful knowledge amid its now outmoded trappings; the alternative is to let such knowledge __________ along with the discredited theories with which it was associated.One object of examining past medical practices is to identify useful knowledge amid its now outmoded trappings; the alternative is to let such knowledge __________ along with the discredited theories with which it was associated.
答案：A, D　fade; wane

答案：E　a costly bottleneck

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　successful for; methodologically rigorous
nonetheless
nonetheless: standard unit headword
#214 SE

答案：B, D　dampen; impede

While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.While many tools can automatically locate patterns in large data sets, they cannot yet distinguish truly significant patterns from those that are (i) __________. Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　merely fortuitous; adept at; spurious

scrutinize: standard unit headword

答案：A　inclusion

Blank 2

It is not just the entry of new collections into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)__________. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)__________ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic

答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristics

discredit: standard unit headword
#254 SE
Historically, the depletion of soil’s nitrogen has been one of the most __________ problems faced by farmers: an essential nutrient, nitrogen is quickly leached from soil, and farmers have struggled to find ways to replenish it.Historically, the depletion of soil’s nitrogen has been one of the most __________ problems faced by farmers: an essential nutrient, nitrogen is quickly leached from soil, and farmers have struggled to find ways to replenish it.
答案：D, E　stubborn; intractable
worrisome
worrisome: standard unit headword
#255 TC DOUBLE
There has been great enthusiasm in the United States for reducing fossil fuel dependence by increasing production biofuels from crops such as corn and switchgrass, but this (i)__________ about biofuels’ potential should be (ii)__________ by a realistic appraisal of the costs and challenges of biofuel production.There has been great enthusiasm in the United States for reducing fossil fuel dependence by increasing production biofuels from crops such as corn and switchgrass, but this (i)__________ about biofuels’ potential should be (ii)__________ by a realistic appraisal of the costs and challenges of biofuel production.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　exuberance; tempered

delineate: standard unit headword
#256 TC TRIPLE

There were few (ii)__________, however, so the form (iii)__________.The modern iron suspension bridge dates from the early nineteenth century, but it did not have (i)__________ debut; many early suspension bridges were damaged, if not outright destroyed, by the wind. There were few (ii)__________, however, so the form (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a propitious; practical alternatives; persisted
conspicuous
conspicuous: standard unit headword

Communicating articulately is typically regarded as an aggressive, persuasive talent, but it can also be protective: it allows a certain __________ closeness, conveying proximity while actually maintaining distance.Communicating articulately is typically regarded as an aggressive, persuasive talent, but it can also be protective: it allows a certain __________ closeness, conveying proximity while actually maintaining distance.
答案：A, E　feigned; false

Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.In reviewing cases decided by lower courts, Supreme Court justices search for precedents to justify their arguments. Reliance on precedent (i)__________ judicial restraint: the precedent (ii)__________ a judge’s ability to determine the outcome of a case in a way that he or she might choose if there were no precedent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　promotes; constraints

#259 TC TRIPLE
Not all paleontologists agree that connections between the continents were (i)__________ just after the extinction of the dinosaurs. Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)__________ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)__________ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.Not all paleontologists agree that connections between the continents were (i)__________ just after the extinction of the dinosaurs. Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)__________ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)__________ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　limited; extensive contacts; similarities

trivial: standard unit headword
#260 SE
According to some political analysts, the candidate’s occasionally rambling responses to questions suggest that she has been out of circulation for a while and her debating skills need to be __________.According to some political analysts, the candidate’s occasionally rambling responses to questions suggest that she has been out of circulation for a while and her debating skills need to be __________.
答案：A, C　honed; enhanced

discredit: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　relish; insufferable

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　preferred; leadership; interchangeable

答案：C, F　obsolete; outmoded

discredit: standard unit headword
#264 TC DOUBLE

As a result, the animal's abundance is often (ii) __________.Among wide-ranging animal species, populations at the edge of the species' range are frequently exposed to less (i) __________ and more variable conditions than those in other parts of the range. As a result, the animal's abundance is often (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　favorable; lower at the periphery

Methods were developed to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Such methods, especially with respect to the researcher, were considered to (i)__________ those (ii)__________ subjectivity whose unbridled expression was thought to otherwise (iii)__________ research.Methods were developed to remove distortions caused by either the research environment or the researcher. Blank 2

答案：A, B　harmless; innocuous

#267 TC DOUBLE

It is part of (ii) __________ in the museum's architecture and design department, which in the past has championed architecture's artistic value over its real-world consequences.The museum's compelling new architectural exhibition looks at eleven projects around the world that have had major (i) __________ impacts despite modest budgets. It is part of (ii) __________ in the museum's architecture and design department, which in the past has championed architecture's artistic value over its real-world consequences.
Blank 1
Blank 2

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　despair over; choreographed; rattle

答案：C, F　prevalent; widespread

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　emergent; half-formulated

But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane

答案：B, E　scant; limited

Blank 2
答案：C, E　technological; germane to

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　hypostatize; did not posit; regarded it as a process

答案：D, F　spurious; specious

Blank 2

Rocke's history of the emergence of the theory of chemical structure encapsulates over 30 years of research on nineteenth-century chemistry. This is (i)__________, since structure theory emerged gradually through the efforts of a network of chemists distributed across several countries. Rocke focuses on just a few key figures, a strategy that necessarily involves some (ii)__________ of detail but that leads to (iii)__________ account of the subject.Rocke's history of the emergence of the theory of chemical structure encapsulates over 30 years of research on nineteenth-century chemistry. This is (i)__________, since structure theory emerged gradually through the efforts of a network of chemists distributed across several countries. Rocke focuses on just a few key figures, a strategy that necessarily involves some (ii)__________ of detail but that leads to (iii)__________ account of the subject.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　no mean feat; sacrifice; a digestible

ploy: standard unit headword

答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

Whether that is a matter of (iii)__________ or a fair reflection of their achievements is debatable.It is a common complaint that female explorers have been unfairly (i)__________ in modern historical accounts. Amelia Earhart, Freya Stark, and Gertrude Bell may be (ii)__________, but only a specialist would recognize the names of Fanny Workman or Janet Wulsin. Whether that is a matter of (iii)__________ or a fair reflection of their achievements is debatable.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　ignored; well enough known; prejudice

答案：C, F　precise; circumspect
