The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.The general statement that all objects are (i)__________ by gravity (ii)__________ is not (iii)__________. In reality, if a feather and a lump of iron were dropped at the same time from the same place, we should not expect them to hit the floor at the same time. In order to reconcile the general statement with real-world phenomena, we must take into consideration all of the forces that act upon objects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　accelerated; at the same rate; self-evident

答案：B, E　prevalent; extensive

It is crucial, however, to (ii)__________. In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i)__________, as near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. It is crucial, however, to (ii)__________. In other words, one needs to resist (iii)__________ generic "manners" with more linguistically and historically precise designations, not least because Austen herself distinguishes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　monolithic; differentiate various behaviors; conflating

Their forms thus necessarily become (i) __________ over time and their original features are (ii) __________.

Blank 2

Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　retain; condemnation of; forgo

答案：B, F　unexceptional; nondescript

Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　Revising; pursue; resolved

答案：B, E　eclipse; outdo

#300 TC DOUBLE
When the United States government created the Post Office at the founding of the republic, it didn't invite rival postal firms to compete; in fact, it created a monopoly. That monopoly, however, was (i) __________ free expression because of policies Congress adopted, which (ii) __________ the circulation of newspapers irrespective of their viewpoint and spread postal service throughout the country.When the United States government created the Post Office at the founding of the republic, it didn't invite rival postal firms to compete; in fact, it created a monopoly. That monopoly, however, was (i) __________ free expression because of policies Congress adopted, which (ii) __________ the circulation of newspapers irrespective of their viewpoint and spread postal service throughout the country.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　conducive to; subsidized

The gravity of the issues discussed requires that we (ii) __________ such (iii) __________: we need publications that engage the broader public in discussions of how we interact with the natural world.Climate science journals are often too hermetic for even the most (i) __________ layperson. The gravity of the issues discussed requires that we (ii) __________ such (iii) __________: we need publications that engage the broader public in discussions of how we interact with the natural world.

Blank 2
Blank 3

The central idea of the worldview known as “consilience” is that all tangible phenomena are based on material processes that are ultimately reducible, however long and __________ the sequences, to the laws of physics.The central idea of the worldview known as “consilience” is that all tangible phenomena are based on material processes that are ultimately reducible, however long and __________ the sequences, to the laws of physics.
答案：C, E　tortuous; convoluted

#303 TC DOUBLE
The notion that scientists consider the work they do to be (i) __________ contradicts popular stereotypes that depict the work of scientists (and the scientists themselves) as being formal and rigid, following lockstep procedures in which the (ii) __________ elements of researchers' personalities fail to enter their labors.The notion that scientists consider the work they do to be (i) __________ contradicts popular stereotypes that depict the work of scientists (and the scientists themselves) as being formal and rigid, following lockstep procedures in which the (ii) __________ elements of researchers' personalities fail to enter their labors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　emotionally satisfying; affective

#304 TC TRIPLE
Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.Several times in this crime series, the police chief presumes herself to be (i) __________, only to learn that, on the contrary, her role is that of (ii) __________: it requires her to (iii) __________ the wishes of her political bosses, who want to hide whatever malfeasance has paralyzed the local police department. At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to
discredit
discredit: standard unit headword
#305 SE
Although extreme and singular events occur in a black hole, its event horizon boundary is __________ space travelers; crossing it would be subject to the same physical laws that apply anywhere else.Although extreme and singular events occur in a black hole, its event horizon boundary is __________ space travelers; crossing it would be subject to the same physical laws that apply anywhere else.
答案：C, F　unremarkable; commonplace
immutable
immutable: standard unit headword
#306 TC DOUBLE
The psychologist's new findings present the discipline with a (i) __________: although her results pose a problem for the standard theory, the alternative theories she proposes are (ii) __________.The psychologist's new findings present the discipline with a (i) __________: although her results pose a problem for the standard theory, the alternative theories she proposes are (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predicament; unconvincing
prototype
prototype: standard unit headword
#307 TC TRIPLE
Unlike those glossy Architectural Digest photographs that are deliberately (i) __________ lest they distract from a focus on the building itself, Friedlander's architectural photography is the product of his insistence that he is an individual looking at buildings and scenes as they actually exist. Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.Unlike those glossy Architectural Digest photographs that are deliberately (i) __________ lest they distract from a focus on the building itself, Friedlander's architectural photography is the product of his insistence that he is an individual looking at buildings and scenes as they actually exist. Friedlander reminds us that everyday objects (ii) __________ or, more exactly, that the unimpeded visual scene of a building without cars or power lines or street signs is a highly (iii) __________ photographic artifact.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　impersonal; normally interfere; contrived
evocative
evocative: standard unit headword
#308 SE
The major __________ of much popular history is that it betrays no interest in making intellectual contributions to our understanding of an issue.The major __________ of much popular history is that it betrays no interest in making intellectual contributions to our understanding of an issue.
答案：B, F　shortcoming; fault
ploy
ploy: standard unit headword
#309 TC DOUBLE
While offering important insights into supernova remnants, Finch's paper is often rather (i) __________: it is (ii) __________ unexplained concepts and details.While offering important insights into supernova remnants, Finch's paper is often rather (i) __________: it is (ii) __________ unexplained concepts and details.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　impenetrable; replete with
trivial
trivial: standard unit headword
#310 TC TRIPLE
As a consequence of his need for (i) __________ and his (ii) __________ self-advertisement, Joseph Duveen's career is better known than those of most of his fellow dealers of museum-quality material. The Wildensteins, for instance, were far more successful financially, but they were (iii) __________ beyond measure.As a consequence of his need for (i) __________ and his (ii) __________ self-advertisement, Joseph Duveen's career is better known than those of most of his fellow dealers of museum-quality material. The Wildensteins, for instance, were far more successful financially, but they were (iii) __________ beyond measure.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　publicity; indefatigable; secretive
autonomy
autonomy: standard unit headword
#311 SE
The idea that Jim was __________, that he loved fighting for the sake of fighting, was a mistake, in reality he was a kind and gentle person.The idea that Jim was __________, that he loved fighting for the sake of fighting, was a mistake, in reality he was a kind and gentle person.
答案：A, E　truculent; pugnacious
audacious
audacious: standard unit headword
#312 TC DOUBLE
Some historians of science maintain that the conceptual gap between successive scientific paradigms is so (i) __________ that scientists adhering to the new paradigm find it quite (ii) __________ to understand what their predecessors could have been thinking.Some historians of science maintain that the conceptual gap between successive scientific paradigms is so (i) __________ that scientists adhering to the new paradigm find it quite (ii) __________ to understand what their predecessors could have been thinking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　great; impossible
trivial
trivial: standard unit headword
#313 TC TRIPLE
Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.Literary fiction is (i) __________ genre fiction. Why? Because its plots are less predictable, its characters deeper, its reality (ii) __________ actual reality. That, at least, is the commonly held view, shared even by readers, and writers, of genre fiction--or so it appears from their readiness to (iii) __________ their tastes. But many new "serious" novels are just as constrained by their genre as any multivolume fantasy epic or cheap romance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　superior to; closer to; apologize for
demanding
demanding: standard unit headword
#314 SE
It may be fairly easy to criticize Tatsumi's comic book on a drawing-by-drawing or line-by-line basis, but one ought not make that mistake, for the book's pleasures are __________.It may be fairly easy to criticize Tatsumi's comic book on a drawing-by-drawing or line-by-line basis, but one ought not make that mistake, for the book's pleasures are __________.
答案：C, F　cumulative; additive
trivial
trivial: standard unit headword
#315 TC DOUBLE
Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　reticent; discretion
demanding
demanding: standard unit headword
#316 TC TRIPLE
Smell is often said to be our most (i) __________ sense, but I am not sure that the evidence really supports that conclusion. Certainly it is (ii) __________ to suggest that primeval organisms learned to discern nice and noxious chemicals before they learned to detect light and sound. Yet there is good evidence to suggest that the smell system we have now is actually (iii) __________ than our visual system and possibly our hearing system as well.Smell is often said to be our most (i) __________ sense, but I am not sure that the evidence really supports that conclusion. Certainly it is (ii) __________ to suggest that primeval organisms learned to discern nice and noxious chemicals before they learned to detect light and sound. Yet there is good evidence to suggest that the smell system we have now is actually (iii) __________ than our visual system and possibly our hearing system as well.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　ancient; reasonable; more recent in origin
credulous
credulous: standard unit headword
#317 SE
The field of urban ecology is in __________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.The field of urban ecology is in __________ phase: just a decade or two ago the idea of doing natural science in a city was something few researchers ever considered.
答案：B, C　an incipient; a nascent
moribund
moribund: standard unit headword
#318 TC DOUBLE
Shark teeth are among the most common of all vertebrate fossils. While the abundance of teeth may (i) __________ creatures renowned for their voracious appetites, it contrasts sharply with the (ii) __________ of shark skeletons in the fossil record.Shark teeth are among the most common of all vertebrate fossils. While the abundance of teeth may (i) __________ creatures renowned for their voracious appetites, it contrasts sharply with the (ii) __________ of shark skeletons in the fossil record.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　befit; rarity
trivialize
trivialize: standard unit headword
#319 TC TRIPLE
Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)__________ the (ii)__________ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)__________, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.Even the reader acquainted with the outlines of Pushkin’s biography will be (i)__________ the (ii)__________ so vividly conveyed in Binyon’s biography. Not only was Pushkin’s personal correspondence intercepted and his movements (iii)__________, but Tsar Nicholas I’s decision to oversee Pushkin’s career obliged Pushkin to submit all his manuscripts for inspection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　struck by; suffocating lack of creative freedom; monitored
interceptunderlie
intercept: standard unit headword | underlie: standard unit headword
#320 SE
Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems __________, not firmly situated in either world.Though Marie Cool and Fabio Balducci consider themselves visual artists rather than performance artists, their work seems __________, not firmly situated in either world.
答案：B, E　liminal; intermediate
prototypical
prototypical: standard unit headword
#321 TC DOUBLE
In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle's corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i) __________ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle's corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i) __________ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to
discredit
discredit: standard unit headword
#322 TC TRIPLE
While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging
conspicuouscounter
conspicuous: standard unit headword | counter: standard unit headword
#323 SE
Recent discoveries suggest that mathematical techniques developed by ancient Babylonian astronomers __________ those of medieval European scholars: Babylonian trapezoid procedures can be viewed as a concrete example of graphical methods devised by fourteenth-century philosopher Nicole Oresme.Recent discoveries suggest that mathematical techniques developed by ancient Babylonian astronomers __________ those of medieval European scholars: Babylonian trapezoid procedures can be viewed as a concrete example of graphical methods devised by fourteenth-century philosopher Nicole Oresme.
答案：D, E　anticipate; prefigure

discredit: standard unit headword
#324 TC DOUBLE
In the face of a fractured polity, the monarchist party attempted to use the notion of a collective national destiny to (i) __________ the differences among the nation's people and thus to (ii) __________ consensus.In the face of a fractured polity, the monarchist party attempted to use the notion of a collective national destiny to (i) __________ the differences among the nation's people and thus to (ii) __________ consensus.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　obfuscate; garner an artificial
delineate
delineate: standard unit headword
#325 TC TRIPLE
The (i)__________ quality of much contemporary drawing may be attributable to the use of photography as a drawing shortcut. Photography (ii)__________ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)__________ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.The (i)__________ quality of much contemporary drawing may be attributable to the use of photography as a drawing shortcut. Photography (ii)__________ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)__________ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　inert; wonderfully enriched; circumvent

proportion: standard unit headword | jubilant: standard unit headword

Attempts to calm down the __________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.Attempts to calm down the __________ toddler proved futile: he fidgeted, hollered, and whined during the entire three-hour plane flight.
答案：B, F　recalcitrant; restive

morose: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　itinerant; peripatetic

#330 TC DOUBLE
High self-esteem has recently assumed a somewhat (i) __________ status in social psychology. On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.High self-esteem has recently assumed a somewhat (i) __________ status in social psychology. On the one hand, high self-esteem is believed to confer a wide variety of adaptive and affective benefits; on the other, positive self-views have been linked to a number of intrapersonal and interpersonal strategies that can reasonably be described as (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　paradoxical; antisocial

The Harvard Observatory took the first picture of a star in 1850, but the complex logistics of photography at the time--involving coating glass plates with chemicals--made it too __________ to photograph stars nightly.The Harvard Observatory took the first picture of a star in 1850, but the complex logistics of photography at the time--involving coating glass plates with chemicals--made it too __________ to photograph stars nightly.
答案：B, D　cumbersome; unwieldy

答案：A, D　attraction; benefit

答案：B, D　relegating; subordinating

#339 SE
Muriel Spark suggested that Mary Shelley's prose style was often verbose and platitudinous, the very opposite of Spark's favored __________.Muriel Spark suggested that Mary Shelley's prose style was often verbose and platitudinous, the very opposite of Spark's favored __________.
答案：E, F　concision; pithiness

#340 SE
It is true that propagandists spout misinformation to serve their own ends, but if our minds were __________ such garbage, the lies would die out quickly.It is true that propagandists spout misinformation to serve their own ends, but if our minds were __________ such garbage, the lies would die out quickly.
答案：A, C　inhospitable to; unreceptive to
amenable
amenable: standard unit headword
#341 SE
The wealth of insightful and challenging articles found in the journal is astounding in a world where mainstream publications increasingly focus on the most __________ of topics.The wealth of insightful and challenging articles found in the journal is astounding in a world where mainstream publications increasingly focus on the most __________ of topics.
答案：A, C　hackneyed; pedestrian
moribund
moribund: standard unit headword
#342 SE
While most Centaurs (minor planets) are thought to be __________, astronomers have observed patterns of brightening from the Centaur Chiron, as well as activity similar to that of a streaking comet.While most Centaurs (minor planets) are thought to be __________, astronomers have observed patterns of brightening from the Centaur Chiron, as well as activity similar to that of a streaking comet.
答案：B, F　quiescent; dormant
immutable
immutable: standard unit headword
#343 SE
Laughter, like speech, is a primarily human faculty, although both of these functions may also exist in a more __________ form in other primates.Laughter, like speech, is a primarily human faculty, although both of these functions may also exist in a more __________ form in other primates.
答案：A, B　rudimentary; elementary
underlie
underlie: standard unit headword
#344 SE
The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.
答案：C, D　belied; contradicted
evoke
evoke: standard unit headword
#345 SE
By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.
答案：A, D　judicious; sensible
solicitous
solicitous: standard unit headword
#346 SE
Leaders within the Native American culture known as Fort Ancient differed from those of the neighboring Mississippian groups, whose authority was often __________; indeed, one expert has described the Fort Ancient society as intensely egalitarian.Leaders within the Native American culture known as Fort Ancient differed from those of the neighboring Mississippian groups, whose authority was often __________; indeed, one expert has described the Fort Ancient society as intensely egalitarian.
答案：D, F　coercive; imperious

答案：A, B　inspirational; animating

答案：A, C　derides; scorns

#349 SE
The problem of not understanding the solar wind, a high-speed stream of particles permeating space around Earth, is not merely __________: explosive solar energy traveling through it threatens astronauts and satellites orbiting Earth.The problem of not understanding the solar wind, a high-speed stream of particles permeating space around Earth, is not merely __________: explosive solar energy traveling through it threatens astronauts and satellites orbiting Earth.
答案：A, D　academic; theoretical

demanding: standard unit headword
#350 SE
Offscreen, actor Madhur Jaffrey is the author of some thirteen books about the cuisine of her native India; therefore, it is not __________ that Indian food was important in her motion picture Chutney Popcorn.Offscreen, actor Madhur Jaffrey is the author of some thirteen books about the cuisine of her native India; therefore, it is not __________ that Indian food was important in her motion picture Chutney Popcorn.
答案：A, E　happenstance; incidental

credible: standard unit headword
#351 SE

答案：B, D　enigmatic; inscrutable
conspicuous
conspicuous: standard unit headword
#352 SE
The cat known to researchers as M-120—beefy, audacious, and apparently smart enough to spot a free lunch—is perhaps the world's least __________ lynx: the scientists catch him several times a year.The cat known to researchers as M-120—beefy, audacious, and apparently smart enough to spot a free lunch—is perhaps the world's least __________ lynx: the scientists catch him several times a year.
答案：B, E　evasive; elusive

#353 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify
